faqs.question: How much does clothes moth control cost in Midtown West?
faqs.answer: Clothes moth control in Midtown West typically ranges from $550 to $5,000 depending on the severity of the infestation, the size of your apartment or home, and the number of treatment visits required. Light infestations in studios or one-bedroom apartments start around $550, while severe infestations in larger spaces with extensive closet areas or multiple rooms can cost up to $5,000. We provide free inspections and detailed quotes before beginning treatment.

faqs.question: What are the signs of a clothes moth infestation in my Midtown West apartment?
faqs.answer: Common signs include small irregular holes in wool, cashmere, silk, or fur clothing; silken tubes or cases in closet corners; adult moths flying near wardrobes (especially at dusk); webbing on fabric surfaces; and shed larval skins in drawers or closets. You may also notice damage to upholstered furniture, rugs, or curtains. Clothes moths prefer dark, undisturbed areas, so check seasonal clothing storage, back corners of closets, and under furniture regularly.

faqs.question: How quickly can you treat a clothes moth problem in Midtown West?
faqs.answer: We offer same-day and next-day service throughout Midtown West for clothes moth emergencies. Initial treatment typically takes 2-4 hours depending on your space size and infestation severity. Most infestations require 2-3 follow-up visits spaced 2-3 weeks apart to target newly hatched larvae and ensure complete elimination. Our technicians will provide a customized treatment schedule during your initial inspection.

faqs.question: Are your clothes moth treatments safe for my family and pets in my Midtown West home?
faqs.answer: Yes, we use EPA-approved products that are safe for families and pets when applied according to label directions. Our treatments focus on targeted application in closets, storage areas, and affected spaces rather than broadcast spraying. We also emphasize non-chemical methods including vacuuming, steam treatment, and pheromone traps. You'll receive specific safety instructions, but generally, treated areas are safe to re-enter within 2-4 hours after application once surfaces have dried.
